_comment: Client definitions. Burgertown is the narrative spine (GDD section 4). contact.flavourLines: 'default' pool plus optional machineType-keyed pools so a line about a specific machine only appears on that machine's tickets; one line is picked deterministically per job. contact.flavour is the legacy single-line fallback.

contact.name: Nina Patel
contact.role: Owner, cashier, and apparently refrigeration department
contact.flavour: If the slushie stops turning, the school run gets personal.

default: If the slushie stops turning, the school run gets personal.
It's making the noise again. The one from last time, but worse.
The kids keep pointing at it. I keep pointing at you. No pressure.

contact.name: Cheryl Voss
contact.flavour: Head office says the machine is fine. The queue says otherwise.

default: Head office says the machine is fine. The queue says otherwise.
Corporate wants a report on why we can't sell frozen desserts. You're the report.
I've got a drive-thru full of regret and a machine full of nothing.

contact.name: Sanjay Kapoor
contact.flavour: Third machine this month. I'm starting to take it personally.

default: Third machine this month. I'm starting to take it personally.
Whatever it's doing, it started at 2 AM. Everything here starts at 2 AM.
commercial-ice-dispenser: If the ice dispenser is down, taxi drivers will riot.
granita-slushie: The granita's gone rogue. Night shift is calling it 'the incident'.

contact.name: Chloe Vance
contact.flavour: Like, the self-serve levers are totally jammed or whatever.

default: The regulars are switching to smoothies. It's becoming a whole thing.
I put up a 'be right back' sign. That was three days ago.
froyo-multihead: Like, the self-serve levers are totally jammed or whatever.
